title: Stimpunks Foundation Micro-Grant
deadline: Opens June 1, 2026 — apply immediately on open

body.text: P31 Labs seeks a $3,000 micro-grant from Stimpunks Foundation to complete the hardware prototyping phase of Node One (Totem) and secure preliminary IP protection. Stimpunks explicitly funds neurodivergent builders working on assistive technology — our project directly serves that mission: an open-hardware, LoRa-connected haptic feedback device for somatic self-regulation, built by an AuDHD operator for autistic families.
NODE ONE — HARDWARE FOR NEURODIVERGENT SOMATIC REGULATION

Node One (codename Totem) is an ESP32-S3-based palm device with DRV2605L haptic engine, SX1262 LoRa radio for off-grid mesh messaging, and NXP SE050 secure element for identity + signing. It provides programmable somatic self-regulation: timed haptic pulses (breathing cues), anxiety alerts, and emergency SOS broadcast to family mesh. The device exists to address a gap: commercial wearables either lock data in walled gardens or require constant phone proximity. Node One works standalone, mesh-only, zero-cloud.
Provisional patent filing × 2 (HERALD algorithm + Node One hardware claims): $130
Copyright registration (group unpublished works, source code + docs): $65
WIPO PROOF timestamps × 5 (priority dates for core IP): $110
DOBE certification (Disability:IN) — supplier diversity channel access: $350
PCB fabrication + components for 5 beta units: $800

All hardware designs (KiCad, Fusion 360), firmware (ESP-IDF), and protocol specifications will be published under CERN-OHL-P-2.0 and GPLv3. We commit to upstreaming any driver improvements to the ESP32 ecosystem.
